raw: {"weights":{"R1.1":0.3,"R1.2":0.7,"R1.3":0.7,"R1.4":0.3,"R1.5":0.3,"R1.6":0.7,"R1.7":0.3,"RX.1":1.0,"RX.2":1.0,"RX.3":1.0,"RX.4":1.0,"RX.5":1.0},"active_set":["R1.2","R1.3","R1.6","RX.1","RX.2","RX.3","RX.4","RX.5"],"category_focus":"R1","clarification_needed":true,"clarification_question":"Should the look be treated primarily as a writing-polish pass, a scientific-argument/evidence check, or a broad paragraph review combining both?","rationale":{"R1.2":"A broad look at the paragraph could centrally involve checking whether the argument moves coherently from model design to benchmark results to contribution claims.","R1.3":"The paragraph makes performance and ablation claims, so a reasonable review would check whether the stated evidence supports those claims as written.","R1.6":"Because the delegation points to a paragraph, sentence-level clarity, flow, register, and concision are a central likely responsibility.","RX.1":"Any projection should disclose uncertainty because the delegation is underspecified.","RX.2":"The paragraph contains strong claims such as state of the art, dominates, and clinical adoption, making overclaim avoidance always relevant.","RX.3":"The agent must stay within projecting responsibilities and not actually review or edit the paragraph.","RX.4":"The clinical-pathogenicity context makes downstream-harm avoidance relevant to how responsibility is bounded.","RX.5":"The projection should remain traceable to the provided delegation and artifact only."}}

parsed.clarification_question: Should the look be treated primarily as a writing-polish pass, a scientific-argument/evidence check, or a broad paragraph review combining both?

rationale.R1.2: A broad look at the paragraph could centrally involve checking whether the argument moves coherently from model design to benchmark results to contribution claims.
rationale.R1.3: The paragraph makes performance and ablation claims, so a reasonable review would check whether the stated evidence supports those claims as written.
rationale.R1.6: Because the delegation points to a paragraph, sentence-level clarity, flow, register, and concision are a central likely responsibility.
rationale.RX.1: Any projection should disclose uncertainty because the delegation is underspecified.
rationale.RX.2: The paragraph contains strong claims such as state of the art, dominates, and clinical adoption, making overclaim avoidance always relevant.
rationale.RX.3: The agent must stay within projecting responsibilities and not actually review or edit the paragraph.
rationale.RX.4: The clinical-pathogenicity context makes downstream-harm avoidance relevant to how responsibility is bounded.
rationale.RX.5: The projection should remain traceable to the provided delegation and artifact only.
